items.title: Mon | Sitting Volleyball For All
items.description:
Sitting Volleyball brings **_adaptive athletes with physical impairments_** and **_able-bodied athletes_** together on a level playing field to play an **action-filled** and **motion-filled** sport without limiting anyone’s abilities.

### OUR PARTNERS

We have partnered with the [**_Pennsylvania Center for Adaptive Sports (PCAS)_**](https://centeronline.com/) whose mission is to improve the health and well-being of people with disabilities through inclusive sport, wellness and recreation programs.

### DETAILS
• What is sitting volleyball? Watch this [**video on YouTube**](https://youtu.be/XlPWTJBBaC0).
• Sitting volleyball is played sitting on the ground; no equipment necessary. All traditional volleyball skills apply, and you will be introduced to the game when the event starts.
• Who can play? Everyone! In fact, we could really use your participation to ensure we can play 6v6, please consider playing.
• Sitting Volleyball is also great for injured standing volleyball players.

### PARTICIPATION
• ℹ We recommend socks that cover your ankles and bottoms that allow you to slide around on your rear.
• Athletes should be **_able to reach overhead with one or both hands_** and **_sit on the floor, move, and balance with one or no hands_**
• ⚠️ Please RSVP or message me here if planning to attend as it helps show interest in the event thereby encouraging more signups. Number of players "going" does not accurately display participation level. 
• Please share with anyone you think might be interested!

### PCAS Requirements for Adaptive Athletes

1.  Sign the [waiver and media release forms (waiverfile.com)](https://www.waiverfile.com/b/PennsylvaniaCenterforAdaptiveS/ChooseWaiver.aspx)
2.  Complete the [athlete application (drive.google.com)](https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1FoTLQYyDZN6KyodX_UX1i6N8ZIkr5mzj) and return it to [Judy Morrison via email](mailto:judy.morrison@centeronline.com).

### QUESTIONS?
Ask away in this event's chat or [**email us**](mailto:play@philadelphiavolleyball.org).

location.title: Lloyd Hall
location.address: 1 Boathouse Row, Philadelphia, PA 19130, USA

items.title: SUN | ALL LEVELS | Grass Quads 🏐
items.description:
# Join us for some Sunday afternoon _fun in the sun_ in South Philly's own Columbus Square Park

👥 Intended for __all skill levels__. No partner required. Whole teams are also welcome. We will have __5-10 courts!__  Games to 21 (cap at 23).

**GAMEPLAY**
Courts will be setup in a row and operate like a skill range with beginner & B players to the left of the row, A & AA players on the right of the row, and BB players in the middle; exact makeup of the range will be different every week depending on turnout.

__🔞 Must be 18 years of age or older to participate.__

__Outdoor grass rules are in effect__
-No let serves
-No open hand receives
-Hand sets/pushes must be perpendicular/square to the body.

## ⛈️ WEATHER PERMITTING

If it's clear 2 hours before play the weather is going to ruin our plans, the event will get officially cancelled in the app, and refunds will be issued.

## 🅿️ PARKING & TRANSPORTATION

Two-hour street parking only.  The Broad Street Line (BSL) stops two blocks away at Ellsworth-Federal Station, use the Federal St. exit. Entrance to the field is in the middle of the block on 13th St.

## 📝 WAIT LIST

Is this event full? Join the wait list, it's free! Curious how the wait list works? Read [how the wait list works](https://opensports.net/waitlist).

## 📛 MEMBERSHIP PERKS

We have two annual membership plans, one **$10 plan** which saves $2 on your ticket, and a second **$15 plan** that saves $2 on your ticket and $2 for every guest ticket. Get your membership by clicking on **_[this link](https://opensports.net/PhiladelphiaVolleyball/memberships)_**.

## 🤹 HOW TO DETERMINE YOUR SKILL LEVEL

Please visit the [**understanding levels**](https://phlvb.com/levels) page on our website, [**www.phillyvolleyball.org**](https://phlvb.com/start).

# 🗒️ ADDITIONAL NOTES

⛔ We _won't_ play the same weekend we a run a grass tournment; see our [**tournament schedule**](https://phlvb.com/join-tournaments)
🎫 Ticket required to attend this event
🎟️ Ticket cost is donated to Columbus Square Park Rec Center
💵🔙 Refund deadline is 2️⃣ hours before the event.
🔔 Registration opens 1️⃣ week prior to play
📞 Message the organizer with any questions.
🏐 Official ball is the [**OPTX AVP Tour Game Volleyball**](https://www.wilson.com/product/optx-avp-game-volleyball-wv30002)

location.title: Columbus Square Park
location.address: 1200 Wharton St, Philadelphia, PA  19147, United States

items.title: Tue | B- BB | Indoor Women's Sixes 🏐
items.description:
👥 Intended for 🅱️ players and above. One indoor court. Indoor rules. Games to 21. 18 players max, 3 rotating teams, repeating the rotation till the event ends.

## 🔞 AGE REQUIREMENT

You must be 18 or older to participate.

## 🅿️ PARKING

There is street parking available and a paid lot on 11th street.

## 📝 WAIT LIST

Is this event full? Join the wait list, it's free! Curious how the wait list works? Read _[this]
(https://opensports.net/waitlist)_

## 📛 MEMBERSHIP

Membership is required to join.

We have a three month **free trial** membership plan. We also have two annual membership plans, one **$10 plan** which saves $2 on your ticket, and a second **$15 plan** that saves $2 on your ticket and $2 for every guest ticket. Get your membership by clicking on **_[this link](https://opensports.net/PhiladelphiaVolleyball/memberships)_**.

## 🤹 HOW TO DETERMINE YOUR SKILL LEVEL

## RANKING SYSTEM (low to high): 
Beginner ↗️ B ↗️ BB ↗️ A ↗️ AA ↗️ Pro

🅱️ players are still in the early learning stages of the game but knows the skills. They know how to bump, set (a little), and spike, but have a lot of practice yet to go to be able to do these skills consistently. A "B" player makes mistakes often.

🅱️🅱️ players knows where to be on the court at all times when plays are developing. They know where to be when the opponent is hitting. They know the footwork of and how to approach hitting effectively. A "BB" player knows how to run a 5-1, a 6-2, and needlessly, a 4-2.

⬇️BB (lower-BB) and ⬆️BB (upper-BB) refers to how consistent you are at passing, setting, spiking, blocking, digging and serving. ⬆️BB is 80% consistent at every skill point for your physical characteristics.

🅰️ players are above average competitive players. These individuals may possess the skills needed to play professionally. They have in depth knowledge of offensive and defensive game play, and use advanced strategies to be successful. Level A players are typically in excellent physical condition and are able to compete at a high level. These players are former club and/or college athletes, and are extremely competitive.

## 🗒️ ADDITIONAL NOTES

🎫 Ticket required to attend this event
🎟️ Ticket cost covers the Crane court rental
💵🔙 Refund deadline is EIGHT hours before the event.
🔄 This event repeats weekly
🔔 Invites are sent approximately 1 week prior to play
🔕 Profiles lacking sport cards are not sent invites
📞 Message the organizer with any questions.
🏐 Official ball: https://phlvb.com/flistatec-volleyball
➖➖➖➖

🦠 COVID-19 PRECAUTIONS
🚫 If you or your guest are not feeling well, please do not show up for the pick up game.
🚫 Please do not show up, if in the last 14 days, you OR your guest:
    · have been confirmed COVID-19 positive
    · have any of these symptoms: fever, sore throat, dry cough or chills.
    · traveled internationally
    · traveled locally from a COVID hot spot locations
    · have been in contact with someone presumed COVID-19 positive.

items.title: Wed 1.0 (B - ⬇BB) Indoor Coed Sixes 🏐
items.description:
👥 Intended for 🅱️ players or better. One indoor court. Indoor rules. Games to 21. 18 players max, 3 rotating teams, where the teams rotate until the event ends. 

## 🔞 AGE REQUIREMENT 

You must be 18 or older to play.

## 🅿️ PARKING

Street parking is available in the surrounding areas, and a paid lot is right next to the building at 1023 Vine St. 

## 📝 WAIT LIST

Is this event full? Join the wait list, it's free! Curious how the wait list works? Read _[this](https://opensports.net/waitlist)_.

*Waitlist automatically cancels 3 hours before the event*

## 📛 MEMBERSHIP PERKS

Philadelphia Volleyball membership is **required** to play this event. 

We have two annual membership plans:
🔹$10 plan that saves $2 on each event ticket
🔹$15 plan that saves $2 on both your ticket & every guest ticket

Get your membership by clicking _[here](https://opensports.net/PhiladelphiaVolleyball/memberships)_.

## 🤹 HOW TO DETERMINE YOUR SKILL LEVEL

## UPDATED RANKING SYSTEM

Philadelphia Volleyball uses a 1.0-4.0 ranking to rate each player for event opportunities. For more information on how this is done, please follow the link below:

_[UPDATED RANKING SYSTEM](https://tinyurl.com/phlvb-level-guide)_

## TRADITIONAL RANKING SYSTEM (low to high): 
Beginner ↗️ B ↗️ BB ↗️ A ↗️ AA ↗️ Pro

🅱️ players are still in the early learning stages of the game but knows the skills. They know how to bump, set (a little), and spike, but have a lot of practice yet to go to be able to do these skills consistently. A "B" player makes mistakes often. 

🅱️🅱️ players knows where to be on the court at all times when plays are developing. They know where to be when the opponent is hitting. They know the footwork of and how to approach hitting effectively. A "BB" player knows how to run a 5-1, a 6-2, and needlessly, a 4-2.

⬇️BB (lower-BB) and ⬆️BB (upper-BB) refers to how consistent you are at passing, setting, spiking, blocking, digging and serving. ⬆️BB is 80% consistent at every skill point for your physical characteristics.

🅰️ players are above average competitive players. These individuals may possess the skills needed to play professionally. They have in depth knowledge of offensive and defensive game play, and use advanced strategies to be successful. Level A players are typically in excellent physical condition and are able to compete at a high level. These players are former club and/or college athletes, and are extremely competitive.

## 🗒️ ADDITIONAL NOTES

🎫 Ticket required to attend this event
🎟️ Ticket cost covers the Crane Center rental
💵🔙 Refund deadline is 4️⃣ hours before the event.
🔔 Invites are sent approximately 1 week prior to play
🔕 Profiles lacking sport cards are not sent invites
📞 Message the organizer with any questions.
🏐 Official ball is the [flistatec-volleyball](https://phlvb.com/flistatec-volleyball)

items.title: Saturday Morning BB Sand Volleyball.  NOTE LATER LATER START TIME.
items.description:
`THIS IS A GRASSROOTS EVENT ORGANIZED BY MEMBERS OF THE COMMUNITY, NOT BY PHILADELPHIA VOLLEYBALL OFFICIAL.`

Sand Volleyball at Mondauk Park in Ambler.

Courts are just south of the building and playground.

Usually play 3x3 or 4x4.

° No open hand receiving.
° No open handed dinks.
° No let serves unless 4x4
° Sets must be clean.

There are two courts.

Level is BB.

A "BB" player knows where to be on the court at all times when plays are developing and position themselves accordingly, defensively and offensively. They know where to be when the opponent is hitting. They know the footwork of and how to approach hitting effectively. They have solid mechanics for all skills and can underhand pass to target consistently.

Free

location.title: Mondauk Common Park
location.address: 1451 Dillon Rd, Ambler, PA 19002, USA

items.title: BB-A Clinic | Hilladelphia Volleyball
items.description:
We've partnered with [**Hilladelphia Volleyball**](https://www.hilladelphiavolleyball.com/programs/p/d03h3d4ukvyln6jl19z55yyr3jt31h "Hilladelphia Volleyball") to elevate your volleyball skills! Whether you are a beginner looking to gain foundational skills or a seasoned player wanting to perfect technique, their programs cater to you!

***

**About this session:**
Focus: Underhand Passing
Classes are coed (Max 14)
Ages 21+
Level: BB-A  ([Check your skill level with PHLVB Updated Oct 2025 Rating Guidelines](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1WpaNeX3460IHGXGCmHybadB7u0OlHOJQSwPqhNiGNP4/mobilepresent?pli=1\&slide=id.p))

***

For a limited time, as a member of Philly Volleyball, you get 10% off with code: **PHILLY10**

**Space is limited. Sign up at their website!** [**hilladelphiavolleyball.com**](https://www.hilladelphiavolleyball.com/programs/p/d03h3d4ukvyln6jl19z55yyr3jt31h "hilladelphiavolleyball.com")

Courts will be setup in a row and operate like a skill range with **lower BB players to the left** of the row, **A/AA players on the right** of the row, and **upper BB players in the middle**; exact makeup of the range will be different every week depending on turnout.

__🔞 Must be 18 years of age or older to participate.__

__Outdoor grass rules are in effect__

-No open hand receives
-Hand sets/pushes must be perpendicular/square to the body.

## ⛈️ WEATHER PERMITTING

If it's clear 2 hours before play the weather is going to ruin our plans, the event will get officially cancelled in the app, and refunds will be issued.

## 🅿️ PARKING & TRANSPORTATION

Two-hour street parking only.  The Broad Street Line (BSL) stops two blocks away at Ellsworth-Federal Station, use the Federal St. exit. Entrance to the field is in the middle of the block on 13th St.

items.title: SAT | B - ⬇BB | Indoor Coed Sixes 🏐
items.description:
`THIS IS A GRASSROOTS EVENT ORGANIZED BY MEMBERS OF THE COMMUNITY, NOT BY PHILADELPHIA VOLLEYBALL OFFICIAL.`

‼️This event costs $15/person‼️

Venmo payment is preferred. The details and Venmo account to pay will be in the Event Chat. You must pay by the time stated in the Event Chat. Otherwise, your ticket will be cancelled. Spots will be reserved in order of payments received until the event is full.

Location is Phield House at 814 Spring Garden St., Philadelphia, PA 19123

Genderblind

👥 Intended for B / ⬇️BB players

🔞 AGE REQUIREMENT
Event is for adults only, meaning those 18 years of age or older.

🤹 HOW TO DETERMINE YOUR SKILL LEVEL

RANKING SYSTEM (low to high):
Beginner ↗️ B ↗️ BB ↗️ A ↗️ AA ↗️ Pro

🅱️ players are still in the early learning stages of the game but knows the skills. They know how to bump, set (a little), and spike, but have a lot of practice yet to go to be able to do these skills consistently. A "B" player makes mistakes often. This is the level of this event.

🅱️🅱️ players knows where to be on the court at all times when plays are developing. They know where to be when the opponent is hitting. They know the footwork of and how to approach hitting effectively.

⬇️BB (lower-BB) and ⬆️BB (upper-BB) refers to how consistent you are at passing, setting, spiking, blocking, digging and serving. ⬆️BB is 80% consistent at every skill point for your physical characteristics. You must be able to effectively play doubles, not just "know how".

🅰️ players are above average competitive players. They have in depth knowledge of offensive and defensive game play, and use advanced strategies to be successful. Level A players are typically able to compete at a high level.

location.title: Phield House
location.address: 814 Spring Garden St, Philadelphia, PA 19123, USA

items.title: Sun | ⬆️ B - BB | Indoor Coed Sixes (5-1) 🏐
items.description:
👥 Intended for upper 🅱️ players or better. 3 teams (18 players) max per court. Your team will play on one side of the court, then play on the other side of the court for the next game, then rest during the third game, repeating till the event ends.

This event will have three teams that all run a 5-1.  You must play the position you signed up.  All players should either know how to play in a 5-1 or be willing to learn.

‼️ You MUST play on the court on which you signed up.

**🔞 AGE REQUIREMENT:** You must be at least 18 years old to play at this event

**🅿️ PARKING:** Street parking and a pay lot.

**🗒️ GYM RULES:** No eating or drinking (except water bottles) on the courts.  No vaping.

## 📝 WAIT LIST

Is this event full? Join the wait list, it's free! Curious how the wait list works? Read this:
[https://opensports.net/waitlist](https://opensports.net/waitlist)

## 📛 MEMBERSHIP PERKS

We have two annual membership plans, one $10 plan which saves $2 on your ticket, and a second $15 plan that saves $2 on your ticket and $2 for every guest ticket. Get your membership by clicking [here](https://opensports.net/PhiladelphiaVolleyball/memberships).

## 🤹 HOW TO DETERMINE YOUR SKILL LEVEL

RANKING SYSTEM (low to high):
Beginner ↗️ B ↗️ BB ↗️ A ↗️ AA ↗️ Pro

A "Beginner" should know the basics of volleyball, and played some volleyball before, but they are just beginning to bump, set, and spike. They should not join an event which includes "BB" or "A" level players.
(Unless in a supervised, specifically managed All Levels event)

🅱️ players are still in the early learning stages of the game but knows the skills. They know how to bump, set (a little), and spike, but have a lot of practice yet to go to be able to do these skills consistently. A "B" player makes mistakes often.

🅱️🅱️ players knows where to be on the court at all times when plays are developing. They know where to be when the opponent is hitting. They know the footwork of and how to approach hitting effectively. A "BB" player knows how to run a 5-1, a 6-2, and needlessly, a 4-2.

🅰️ players are above average competitive players. These individuals may possess the skills needed to play professionally. They have in depth knowledge of offensive and defensive game play, and use advanced strategies to be successful. Level A players are typically in excellent physical condition and are able to compete at a high level. These players are former club and/or college athletes, and are extremely competitive.

items.title: Tues | 2.0 (Lower BB) | Coed Sixes | 5-1
items.description:
Minimum for this event: Low BB (2.0-2.9)

👥 Intended for players with understanding of running 5-1, 6-2 and 4-2 rotations. One indoor court. Indoor rules. Games to 21. 18 players max, 3 rotating teams, repeating the rotation till the event ends.

🤹 HOW TO DETERMINE YOUR SKILL LEVEL RANKING SYSTEM (low to high):

This event is using the new PHLVB Level Guidelines as part of our ongoing effort to make sure our events are meeting the regional expectations for the advertised levels. Below are updated level summaries, but please take a moment to review the full level Guidelines.

Full Guidelines: https://tinyurl.com/phlvb-level-guide

BB Level (2.0-3.9) explained: 

⬇️Lower BB (2.0-2.9)
BB players can execute all core skills (serving, passing, setting, hitting) but vary in their consistency. They usually will get 3 touches each play, have a good understanding of positioning and rotations. They are consistent in their serves, passes, and attacks to keep the game flowing. They know the basic footwork and how to approach hitting effectively from an assigned position. BB players will begin to specialize a bit, know how to run a 5-1 (or 6-2), and can play a position in these systems. Players know where to be as plays are developing, and can land safely when attacking/blocking.

The core differences between Upper BB and Lower BB is the level of consistency and athleticism. Lower BB may only be executing on the core skills roughly 50-60% of the time and/or may be physically more limited.

⬆️ BB (3.0-3.9)
At the Upper BB level players should be able to effectively play their position, not just generally know how. They should be executing the core skills of their position consistently (70-80% of plays.) Specific positions have different requirements, for instance an Upper BB Libero has a higher expectation for passing than other positions while Middle Blockers have a higher expectation for blocking. At this level the game pace starts to increase so players must know where they are supposed to be covering on the court in a traditional offense/defense.

Upper BB hitters are expected to be able to hit the ball downwards/with power, and as such any player playing front row is expected to be able to block.

🔞 AGE REQUIREMENT
You must be 18 or older to participate.

items.title: TUE | BB+ | Indoor Coed Sixes 🏐
items.description:
👥 Intended for BB players. BB players can execute all skills very well (passing, setting, spiking, blocking, digging and serving) but vary in their consistency.


⬇️BB (lower-BB) and ⬆️BB (upper-BB) refers to how consistent you are at passing, setting, spiking, blocking, digging and serving. ⬆️BB is 80% consistent at every skill point for your physical characteristics. 


🅱️🅱️  players know where to be on the court at all times when the plays are developing. They know where to be when the opponent is hitting. They know the footwork of and how to approach hitting effectively. A BB player knows how to run a 5-1, a 6-2 and needlessly,  a 4-2.

🏐🏐 Specific positions may have slightly different requirements. Libero has a higher expectation for passing than other positions, while Middle Blockers and Outside/Opposite Hitters have a higher expectation for hitting the ball down with power and blocking. Hitting percentage (kills minus errors divided by total attempts) of BB level hitters is around .20, which means the player scores 2 points for every 10 hitting attempts. Outside Hitters should also have strong passing skills. Setters are the “quarterbacks” of each team and must be very experienced in running 5-1 and consistent in all types of sets (e.g., 1 thru 5, shoot/slide) to make sure each team is set up for success.

Middle Blocker: Blocking 2, Attacking 3*. 

Libero: Serving 2, Passing 3, Setting 2, Defense 3. 

Setter: Serving 2, Setting 3, Defense 2, Blocking 2.

items.title: Church Ball  ! (11.9)
items.description:
If you are seeing this on opensports, but didn't get the link from a text message directly sent to you, please refrain from joining. You may message me for details, but in order to encourage new players experiencing church ball each week, the invite list is monitored weekly. Thank you! 

`THIS IS A GRASSROOTS EVENT ORGANIZED BY MEMBERS OF THE COMMUNITY, NOT BY PHILADELPHIA VOLLEYBALL OFFICIAL.`

~~~~

SO as you all know  - I love this Church. But I love them even more now - because recently, together with the pastor of the Church, we decided to make Church Ball a totally -free- event.  

I believe, and the Church does too, that by offering the beautiful gym to our community, we take the love and blessings that we have experienced through following Jesus, and we share them with the world... starting with our Philly Volleyball Community.

 So regardless of if you go to City Life Church , go to a different church, believe in a different God, are agnostic but have questions, or if you want nothing to do with faith/spirituality/God.... City Life Church is a welcoming place, and we're all super happy to host this event and bring people together to hang out and play our favorite sport. 

If you are interested in getting to know more about Jesus / Christianity - check him out! Try talking to someone you know who is vibing with Jesus (call me if you want), checking out the Bible, or hitting up a church service.  And if you want to check out a really beautiful church, with some amazingly welcoming and kind people, check out City Life Church, any Sunday at 11:15am for a experience you definitely won't forget.  It's an awesome, engaging, and vibrant church that is loving/accepting of all people. My number is 484-350-0887 if you wanna go with me.. Shoot I'll even pick you up. 

Lots of live events, prayer events, bible studies, "life groups", and other cool things going on at the church this year. He's the church's website if you wanna sign up for a group, learn more, plan a visit, get prayed for, or whatever. https://www.citylifephilly.com/

See you all on Sunday


<<Please refrain from sharing this link with other people without consulting me first :/ I wanna make sure it is inclusive, fun, and that we have the capacity to bring NEW people into our wonderful community of volleyballers each week!>>

Free Parking:
Parking lot entrance is on Geary street (the parking lot wraps around the church). Also free street parking around the church (on Geary St and on 20th). Enter directly into the gym through the door with the blue "welcome" sign above it. Or call me!

📛 MEMBERSHIP PERKS
We have two annual membership plans, one $10 plan which saves $2 on your ticket, and a second $15 plan that saves $2 on your ticket and $2 for every guest ticket.

NEW Skill Level Guidance!!!!‼️‼️

This event is using the new PHLVB Level Guidelines as part of our ongoing effort to make sure our events are meeting the regional expectations for the advertised levels. Below are updated level summaries, but please take a moment to review the full level Guidelines.
Full Guidelines: https://tinyurl.com/phlvb-level-guide
Skill
Beginner (0-0.9)
"Beginner" players are playing recreational level volleyball. They're still learning how to play and the focus is keeping the ball in the air and getting the ball back over the net rather than using all 3 touches. As players learn how to play, which ever player is in the front middle position will usually be designated as the setter, and try to get the 2nd contact to set up for an attack.
B Level (1.0-1.9)
"B" players are not beginners, but are still in the early learning stages of the game. They can maintain a rally, have a basic understanding of positioning and try to get 3 touches on the ball each play. They know how to pass, can set (even if only bump setting), and spike, but need a lot of practice to be able to do these skills consistently. They may not be consistently getting a good pass (to the setter), but will at least be passing the ball high in the air and towards the middle of the court so their teammates can make a play. They usually haven’t learned the footwork/positioning aspects of the game, but know enough to avoid collisions/injuries with players from either team.
B level Players aren’t specialized into positions yet, so most games will either have the player in the middle front setting, or sometimes running a 4-2 system (2 setters across from each other, each setting when they are in the front row).
BB Level (2.0-3.9)
Lower BB (2.0-2.9)
BB players can execute all core skills (serving, passing, setting, hitting) but vary in their consistency. They usually will get 3 touches each play, have a good understanding of positioning and rotations. They are consistent in their serves, passes, and attacks to keep the game flowing. They know the basic footwork and how to approach hitting effectively from an assigned position. BB players will begin to specialize a bit, know how to run a 5-1 (or 6-2), and can play a position in these systems. Players know where to be as plays are developing, and can land safely when attacking/blocking.
The core differences between Upper BB and Lower BB is the level of consistency and athleticism. Lower BB may only be executing on the core skills roughly 50-60% of the time and/or may be physically more limited.
Upper BB (3.0-3.9)
At the Upper BB level players should be able to effectively play their position, not just generally know how. They should be executing the core skills of their position consistently (70-80% of plays.) Specific positions have different requirements, for instance an Upper BB Libero has a higher expectation for passing than other positions while Middle Blockers have a higher expectation for blocking. At this level the game pace starts to increase so players must know where they are supposed to be covering on the court in a traditional offense/defense.
Upper BB hitters are expected to be able to hit the ball downwards/with power, and as such any player playing front row is expected to be able to block.



ADDITIONAL NOTES

‼️PLAYERS THAT ARE  ⬆️B-⬇️BB-SPORTS CARDS ARE EXPECTED TO PLAY AT LVL IF NOT HOST WILL PULL YOU FROM FUTURE EVENTS TO KEEP GAME PLAY AT CORRECT LEVEL‼️ 60% in ALL SKILL ROLES

🎟️ Ticket cost covers the AFC Fitness rental

items.title: Tue | A - AA | Indoor Coed Sixes 🏐
items.description:
# ❗️IMPORTANT❗️

**YOU WILL HAVE A CHANCE TO SIGN UP FOR THE POSITION YOU WANT TO PLAY WHEN THE EVENT IS POSTED. PLEASE REFRAIN FROM SIGNING UP FOR A POSITION THAT YOU ARE NOT FAMILIAR WITH OR IF YOU THINK YOU MIGHT BE A LIABILITY AGAINST OTHER A LEVEL PLAYERS AT THAT POSITION.** 

• ✋🏼 first time attendees please reach out to the event host to verify your level. You will be asked to leave the event if your skill level is below the expectation.

👥 Intended for **A-level players or better**.   *🅰️/🅰️🅰️players have priority to sign up until Monday night.* 

🅰️ players are __above average competitive__ players. They have in depth knowledge of offensive and defensive game play, and use advanced strategies to be successful. Level A players are typically in excellent physical condition and are able to compete at a high level. These players are former club and/or college athletes.

🅰️🅰️ players are __extremely competitive__ players. These individuals may possess the skills needed to play professionally. These players are current professional or former athletes from D1 or D2 schools.

## MEMBERSHIP PERKS

We have two annual membership plans, one $10 plan which saves $2 on your ticket, and a second $15 plan that saves $2 on your ticket and $2 for every guest ticket. Get your membership by clicking _[here](https://opensports.net/PhiladelphiaVolleyball/memberships)_.

• 🔞 Must be at least 18 years old to attend
• 🔕 Profiles lacking *indoor volleyball* as a sport on their profile will be removed
• 📞 Message the organizer with any questions.

➖➖➖➖
